whoUses: fictional confrontation scenes only — movies, anime, games (役割語/yakuwarigo)
usageContext: A stock warning line when someone has disrespected or underestimated the speaker. Combines the ねえ contraction, ぞ ending, and contracted じゃない→んじゃねえ all in one line — a compact example of several grammar points in this pack at once.
warningNotes: Media-only register — reciting this in a real confrontation would sound bizarre, not intimidating, to an actual Japanese speaker.

examples.text: 舐めてんじゃねえぞ、コラ。
examples.reading: なめてんじゃねえぞ、コラ。
examples.translation: Don't you dare underestimate us, hey!

natural: 'get ready' / 'you'd better be prepared' — a stock threat
usageContext: The imperative of 覚悟する, used as a classic pre-confrontation warning line — telling someone to brace for consequences.
warningNotes: Recognize-in-media only — an ordinary threat register, not something a learner should ever have occasion to say for real.

examples.text: 逃げられると思うなよ、覚悟しろ。
examples.reading: にげられるとおもうなよ、かくごしろ。
examples.romaji: Nigerareru to omou na yo, kakugo shiro.
examples.translation: Don't think you can run — get ready.

natural: 'make this right' / 'you're going to pay for this'
usageContext: Demanding someone answer for a debt, insult, or broken promise — one of the most iconic lines of the genre, built directly on the 落とし前 vocabulary entry.
saferAlternatives: きちんと責任を取ってください
warningNotes: Severe, genuinely threatening register even within this already-rough pack — media recognition only.

examples.text: うちの若い衆に手を出した落とし前、つけてもらうぞ。
examples.reading: うちのわかいしゅにてをだしたおとしまえ、つけてもらうぞ。
examples.romaji: Uchi no wakaishuu ni te o dashita otoshimae, tsukete morau zo.
examples.translation: You're going to answer for laying a hand on my men.

whoUses: fictional confrontation scenes, but the underlying phrase 手を出す is also plain ordinary Japanese in non-threatening contexts
usageContext: A stock protective/warning line — often said defending a shatei or katagi bystander. Unlike most entries here, 手を出す itself is completely ordinary vocabulary (to get involved with / to lay a hand on); it's the blunt な imperative and context that make this line confrontational.
saferAlternatives: 関わらないでください

examples.text: この人はカタギだ。手を出すな。
examples.reading: このひとはカタギだ。てをだすな。
examples.romaji: Kono hito wa katagi da. Te o dasu na.
examples.translation: This person is a civilian. Don't touch them.

phrase: ただで済むと思うなよ
reading: ただですむとおもうなよ
romaji: tada de sumu to omou na yo
literal: don't think it will end for free
natural: 'you won't get off easy' / 'this isn't over'
usageContext: A stock closing threat, often the last line before a confrontation scene cuts away — warns of unspecified future consequences.

examples.text: 今日のところは帰してやる。だが、ただで済むと思うなよ。
examples.reading: きょうのところはかえしてやる。だが、ただですむとおもうなよ。
examples.romaji: Kyou no tokoro wa kaeshite yaru. Daga, tada de sumu to omou na yo.
examples.translation: I'll let you go for today. But don't think this is over.
